description: This group includes two scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images of fracture surfaces, both equipped with a 20 μm scale bar. The first image presents the fracture surface of a Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C austenitic lightweight steel specimen with 1.07 wt.% Si, which was aged at 530 °C for 12 h and fractured at -40 °C. The second image shows the fracture surface of a 1.56 wt.% Si specimen prepared under identical aging and fracture testing conditions. Both specimens exhibit dominant transgranular cleavage-like brittle fracture features, with distinct slip traces observed on favorably oriented cleavage facets for both samples. No dramatic difference in fracture morphology is visually distinguished between the two samples with different Si contents. The observed cleavage-like fracture characteristics verify the low-temperature brittle fracture mechanism of the aged high Mn-Al steel, which is related to glide plane decohesion induced by κ-carbide precipitation and planar slip, supporting the conclusion that higher Si addition reduces the low-temperature notch toughness of aged Fe-Mn-Al-C steels.
